top_rant.text:
For some reasons, Amazon.com is allowing only one review for ALL Lego Nexo Nights Ultimate Sets. That is the reason that I’m writing/updating all reviews for the Nexo Knights Ultimate Sets, that I currently own (which are “Ultimate Axl”, “Ultimate Aaron” , “Ultimate Clay” , "Ultimate Lance","Ultimate Macy" ,"Ultimate Lavaria" , "Ultimate Beast Master" , "Ultimate General Magmar" and "Ultimate Flama"), in one single review, in sections with relevant headings.
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
REVIEW FOR “ULTIMATE FLAMA” , Set no 70339
Rating : 4 Stars
This set contains the minifigure Flama, some weapons and accessories and a display stand.
Flama looks awesome! Great printing on his torso and head. The lower part of his body (whatever it’s called) is nicely done in transparent orange with bit of yellow. His head gives a great effect, with a transparent orange piece that completely goes over the yellow standard LEGO headpiece.
He carries a large hammer attached to his back. There is lever on his back, which when turned will rotate the hammer back and front. The play feature is nice, more suited for kids. The hammer looks bit mediocre though. The spiked-piece at it’s back keeps falling off frequently.
You get three printed shields/Nexo powers which look pretty good.
The display stand , once everything is assembled on it, looks neat.
When it comes to parts, this set is like a small gold mine. I mean, it contains fantastic (new) parts! My favorite is the long spiked-piece which you get in TWO colors in this set. (One black and three white). It’s the first time I’m getting this piece and I feel this is one of the best parts LEGO has created. In fact, this part should have been created many years ago.It is best used as the end of a spear, top of a tower and many other uses.
To wrap up, this is a very good set. One fo the best small-sized sets from LEGO. I’m buying to buy more of this. I love the Flama minfigure and the parts in this set.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
REVIEW FOR “ULTIMATE GENERAL MAGMAR” , Set no 70338
Rating : 4 Stars
I’m addicted to the Nexo Knights Cartoon show and General Magmar is one of my favorite character in the show. So I just HAD to buy this set. And I was more happy when I found the set to be more-than-satisfying.
This set contains General Magmar minifigure, some weapons and accessories and a display stand.
The minifigure is AWESOME, to say the least! I have rarely seen such beautiful printing on a minifigure! His face had excellent printing (front and back); very expressive. Even his torso (front and back) has fabulous printing! He has a metallic visor/mask ,around his face, which looks fantastic !
He carries a attachment on his back which is a set of two rotating circular blades. Both of them have a lever on the end, which when turned, will rotate the blades. A nice feature. My minor complaint about the attachment is that it’s way TOO BIG for the minifigure and it also covers the beautiful printing on the back of the minifigure. (I wish LEGO would stop making attachments too big for a minifigure; it looks ridiculous,clumsy, weird).
You get three printed shields/Nexo powers which look fantastic! Great new accessories from LEGO. These shields are one of the main reasons for me to buy Nexo Knights sets!
The display stand, once you arrange everything as required, looks very nice indeed.
Another reason I love this set is that you get a lot of great parts! Like the transparent red blade-like piece , used in General Magmar’s default weapon. The big transparent orange lava-like piece, used in the centre of the display stand is nice. The transparent orange circular blades are nice. Even the transparent blue propeller-like pieces are cool. My favorite part is the black shield-holder which is fantastic. (It’s even better than the grey shield-holder that you get for the Knights in other sets).
To wrap up, this is a beautiful set. Awesome-looking minifigure and great weapons,accessories and parts. Definitely a great gift for kids! I’m surely going to buy a lot more of this set! Just for the General Magmar minifigure! Good to see a small set from LEGO that is a great value for money!

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
REVIEW FOR “ULTIMATE BEAST MASTER” , Set no 70334
Rating : 4 Stars
This set contains the minifigure Beast Master with some weapons, accessories and a display stand.
Beast Master looks pretty good. The printing on his face (two-sided) is fantastic! But I didn’t like the printing on his chest and legs because of too many Globlin-logos!
His drill dagger weapon is very good. His black shield-holder is a fabulous new piece introduced in this theme (It’s even better than the grey shield-holder that you get for the Knights in other sets).
The attachment on his back, with the rotating chains, is way too big for him. (I wish LEGO would STOP making attachments/weapons that are too big for a minifigure. It looks clumsy, awkward and ridiculous).Also, when you move the lever and the chains start moving, either they hit your finger or the minifigure’s head, which is irritating.
The three printed shields/Nexo powers look magnificent. They are one of the best accessories LEGO has introduced and also one of the main reason that I’m buying Nexo Knights sets. But one minor complaint I have is that the names of these Nexo powers are not mentioned in the box or instruction manual; I had to look up the internet to find out their names.
There are still some more cool accessories in this set. Like the big transparent orange lava-like piece, used in the centre of the display stand, is fantastic. Even the printed Force Field shield is marvelous.
The two Globlins, that are attached at the end of the two chains, are some of my favorite accessories/parts in this theme.
The display stand , after everything is added to it, looks pretty nice.
Overall, I’m more than happy with this set. Great minifigure, fantastic parts. This theme, Nexo Knights, itself is the BEST theme that LEGO has released so far, in my opinion.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
REVIEW FOR “ULTIMATE LAVARIA” , Set no 70335
Rating : 4 Stars
This rating is actually 4.5 stars.
This set contains the minifigure Lavaria and some weapons and accessories.
Lavaria is one of my favorite characters in the “Nexo Knights” cartoon show so I just HAD to buy this set. And the fact that this set is simply fantastic, in many aspects, made me double happy!
Starting with the main part, the minifigure Lavaria is very pretty! Her face printing is very expressive and nicely done. The printing on her chest is simply awesome; and good on her legs too.
The assembled weapon that she carries is nice but it’s bit big for her.
There are three printed shields/Nexo powers which look fantastic! These parts are one of the main reasons I’m buying the Nexo Knights sets.
This set contains some parts that I’m seeing for the first time. The big transparent orange lava-rock-like part , used in the centre of the display stand, is simply …fabulous ! The crossbow stud shooter is awesome! (Wish LEGO would make more weapons like these). The black shield-holder looks awesome, primarily because of it’s rocky outline! (It’s a lot better than the grey shield-holder that you get for the good guys/Knights). The spider-legs contraption contain four number of a part, which is another fantastic looking piece.
To wrap up, this is an awesome set from LEGO that is worth the money. ‘m surely buying some more of this particular set. I love everything about this set. In the six years that I’m buying LEGO I’ve NEVER seen so many new and awesome parts in ANY other theme! I’m glad that currently I’m buying only NEXO Knights theme. LEGO has taken this theme to the next level…in all aspects. Seriously.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
REVIEW FOR “ULTIMATE MACY” , Set no 70331
Rating : 4 Stars
This set contains the minifigure Macy , three Nexo powers and some weapons , accessories and attachments.
Macy looks stunningly beautiful in red! Her red transparent armor and visor are fantastic. You get a hair piece ( tuft of hair, actually ) that you can attach to top of the visor and it looks very nice. Printing on her is very good. Sadly no printing on the sides of torso and legs. Wish there was. (In the Nexo Knights cartoon show, there is printing on the sides of the torsos and legs of the Knights.LEGO should have done the same for these minifigs).
Macy’s mace is a great little build. It contains five pyramid-like bricks, which look awesome. I’m seeing this brick for the first time (It was introduce in this theme). And you get TWO extra in this set, which is a good thing that LEGO did. Even the shield-holder piece (or whatever it’s called) is very good. You get three printed shields /Nexo powers in this set. I love these printed shields. It’s a great accessory. And also one of the main reasons I’m going to continue buying this theme sets.
You get a six-stud-shooter build that you can attach to Macy’s back but the build is really HUGE for a minifigure and it looks very awkward on her.
You get some few parts to represent each of the three Nexo powers like some vines and also some different colored studs for the stud-shooter. You also get a cool-looking treasure chest , with transparent orange lid for storing extra studs.
To wrap up, this is a buy-worthy set that contains a great looking minifigure and some very nice bricks.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
REVIEW FOR “ULTIMATE LANCE” , Set no 70337
Rating : 4 Stars
This set contains the minifigure Lance and some weapons/attachments/accessories.
Lance looks fabulous! Great printing on him. His transparent white armor is very cool and his transparent white visor is fantastic. Wish there was side printing on his torso and legs. That would have been awesome! (In the Nexo Knights Cartoon show, not only Lance, but all the other four Knights have side printing on their torso and legs.) Generally I don’t like assembled weapons in LEGO but the one in this set, a lance, looks pretty good, especially because of a transparent round brick and two transparent gems.
The three printed shields/ Nexo powers are very good! Even the shield-holder brick (or whatever it is called) is very good.
I like the two white wing pieces that are used as an attachment on Lance’s back. It’s the first time I’m getting these pieces and I like them a lot. Generally I didn’t like any of the attachment for the other four Ultimate Knights’ sets but this one is pretty decent-looking. Even the stud-shooter-assembled weapon is cool.
To wrap up, this is another good Ultimate Nexo Knight set. Great minifigure and very cool parts.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
REVIEW FOR “ULTIMATE CLAY” , Set no 70330
Rating : 4 Stars

This set contains the minifigure Clay and quite a few weapons and accessories.
Clay looks awesome, to say the least! In fact he looks LOT better than the in pictures and video review of the set. The shade of blue on his costume is very appealing. His transparent blue armor is very nice. His transparent blue visor is fantastic. Great printing on him. But wish there was printing on the side of his torso and legs. Then it would have been more awesome! In the Nexo Knights Cartoon show, there is side printing on torso and legs of all five Nexo Knights. Wish LEGO would have done the same for the minifigures.
The three shield/Nexo powers in this set look excellent.One of the best accessories introduced by LEGO in a long time.( These shields are one of the main reasons I’m buying the Nexo Knights Sets).
This set contains five transparent blue sword blades which are very cool.
The attachment on Clay’s back is bit mediocre. Even the other two attachments are bit disappointing.
To wrap up, this is a great set because of the awesome minifigure and some cool parts/bricks.
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
REVIEW FOR "ULTIMATE AXL"
(This rating is actually 4.5 stars)
This is my first “Nexo Knights” set. Before buying this set, I thought it’s good,but it turned out to be…great! After a very long time, a LEGO set has made me extremely happy! This set turned out to be a LOT better than it’s pictures and video reviews.
The set contains the Axl minifigure, three scannable shields, three weapons and a showcase/display stand.
The box has a nice image. Generally I throw the LEGO boxes but I’ll keep this one.
The Axl minifiure is just AWESOME! He’s one of THE best minifigures from LEGO. HE’s already in my list of favorite minifigures. He looks fantastic in orange color. Great printing on him. His helmet and torso design is magnificent! (His torso is not the regular minifigure one, but a large one and that is one of the main reasons for his appeal).Even his transparent visor piece is fantastic.Wish all the other Knights in this theme also had this kind of torso  I am VERY happy that LEGO made included Axl in a”Ultimate” set. In the first wave of “U,timate NExo Knights”, Axl was not included. I was disappointed as I didn’t want to buy a huge/costly set just to get this minifigure. But fortunately my prayers were answered. Now, not only can you get Axl in a cheap set, he’s also a LOT better looking than the Axl you get in the three big and huge sets (“Fortrex Castle”,”Axl’s Tower Carrier” and “Jestro’s volcanic lair”). So thanks to LEGO.
There is a small attachment that you fix to Axl’s back. It contains two fists that move front and back when you move a small know. A nice feature.
There are three weapons (an axe, a magnetizer club and a spinning top) in this set.
You get three printed shields called “Nexo powers” (that you can scan using some application and use them in the game). These shields look simply fantastic! Great new accessories from LEGO! One minor complaint I have is that the names of these shields/Nexo powers were not mentioned either on the box or on the instruction manual. I had to look up the official description on LEGO’s website to find their names.
The display stand , though small, is very appealing. Once you put everything on it, the look is just…fantastic!! I’ve seen quite a few display stands from LEGO in the past and this one is one of the best. It even gives a feel of what this theme represents and the kind of parts it contains.
Apart from Axl, the main reason I liked this set is because of quite a bit of new parts and accessories included in this set. As I already said, the printed shields are fabulous. The shield-holder part (or whatever it’s called) is great. The big triangular part (on which Axl is placed on the stand) is great. (one of the best new parts created from LEGO in quite some time). The visor piece is nice. The transparent yellow axe-blade piece (which you get 5!) is great.
The reasons that I gave this product a 4.5 rating and not 5 is because:
1) I didn’t like the moving-fists attachment on his back. I think it spoils his look. Though it’s a good feature it looks bit awkward. I guess it was specifically designed for younger kids so maybe I shouldn’t complain.
2) The two weapons, magnetizer club and spinning top, are bit mediocre. They could have been designed better or maybe some new weapon pieces could have been created.
To wrap up, this set is a winner. Something anyone and everyone will love! And because of this set, my interest in the “Nexo Knights” them has piqued!
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
REVIEW FOR "ULTIMATE AARON"
Rating: 3 Stars
The first Ultimate Nexo Knights set that I bought was “Ultimate Axl”. That was an awesome set, especially because of the minifigure. And since I liked that set, I decided to buy other Ulimate Nexo Knights sets. This set I am a little disappointed.
This set contains the minifigure Aaron, with some weapons, accessories and shields.
Aaron looks quite good.Nice printing on him. His green transparent armor and visor are very cool. I wish there was printing on the side of the torso and legs.Then the minifig would have looked awesome! (Actually in the Nexo Knights cartoon show, Aaron , and the other Nexo Knights, have printing on the sides of the torso and legs. Sad that LEGO didn’t implement the same on the minifigures).
His default weapon is a assembled-bow. It looks nice. Has a great shooting feature. But one major problem is that is oversized for the minifigure. Even his shield, which looks very nice is also a bit big. His backpack, which also serves as an attachment to carry two stud shooters is also big. Once you equip Aaron with all the weapons and attachments as shown in the box image, he becomes overloaded! In fact, you can barely see him because of the weapons and accessories. You can't even move the weapons properly after putting them in both the hands of the minifig. I've been buying LEGO sets since many years and I've become sick of frequently getting parts/accessories that are too big for the minifig. The LEGO designers either have no common sense or no sense of proportion. LEGO sets are not exactly cheap and I don't like getting parts I can't use. I just hope in the future, LEGO completely stops making over-sized parts. It's very rare but sometimes I have seen LEGO make some positive changes in it's sets.
Anyway. Apart from the minifigure, another reason I like the set is because of some new parts introduced in this theme. Like the shields/Nexo powers. You get three in this set, which is very nice. But sadly there is no mention of the names of these Nexo powers , either on the box or in the instruction manual. I had to look up the net for their names. There are also four bananas in this set, which is also very cool.
You get a black helmet which looks completely out of theme.
Despite a few dislikes, I would STILL recommend this set. Reasons: a co looking minifig, some very good parts/bricks and some nice shooting features.
top_rant.title: One of the BEST sets from LEGO!!

top_rant.text:
Originally posted at Throat Punch Games, a new idea every day!

Product- Shadowrun 6th Ed.
System-Shadowrun 6th Ed.
Producer- Catalyst Game Lab
Price- $39.99
TL; DR- New but similar. 93%

Basics- Shadowrun is 30 years old! In honor of this, here is the 6th edition. What changed? What's new? What's old? Let's dive in together!

Basics- Shadowrun 6th ed. uses the solid base of 5th edition. Almost all rolls are: take an attribute (the stats of your character that range mostly between 1 to 6), add your skill ranks, and roll that many dice. You then count the number of 1s and the 5s and 6s. 5 and 6 are successes and used to determine if you succeed, while if you have over half 1s, that's a glitch and something bad happens. Pretty simple.

What's new with the basics-the BIGGEST change between editions is edge. Edge is the magic cheaty story currency. You buy pizza? Get a point of edge. You roleplay well? Edge. You're the only player paying attention and you catch the plot hook? EDGE! What changed is now before every roll we check who has an advantage in a situation. That side gains edge now. You see in the dark and the opponent doesn't? Edge for you! What's also changed is edge isn't just rerolls now. It's much more an a la carte menu where you choose abilities, to change dice faces, and other crazy options. Edge even powers new abilities.

Combat-combat got more of a face lift with two sizable, but manageable changes. 1st, armor...is gone. In 5th ed, I would roll to hit you. You would roll to dodge. If I got more successes (5s and 6s on the dice) than you then the difference would be added to the weapon damage. You would then roll a soak roll. Soak is reducing damage, and it was found by adding your body attribute to your armor value. You would roll that many d6s praying for 5s and 6s so you would not die! In 6th ed, armor doesn't provide damage reduction but instead provides a score to compare to guns to gain edge. It means you roll much fewer dice and weapon damage is also down to compensate. The second biggest change is actions in combat. Before, in 5th, you would roll initiative and all characters would act from high to low. After actions, you would subtract 10 from all scores, and characters with scores above 0 would act again. This would result in multiple passes for fast characters moving at super human speeds. Now all characters roll initiative as before, but depending on the number of d6s you roll you may gain additional actions. Every character gets 1 minor action (things like run ) and 1 major action (thinks like attack and cast spells). You can spend several minor actions to make a second major action. This means you get fewer passes at the top of the round, but you also have a more stable initiative action order and action amounts.

Magic- magic changes, but it still follows the basics above. Before you would choose how hard a spell would hit, now you just roll and if you get more hits, you increase magic effectiveness after the roll doing more damage or being more hidden based on your result. Magic's overall power did go down, but it also gained some significant ease of use, especially in regard to healing!

Technology-All the basics you loved before are here except streamlined. You no longer need to MARC (put a tag on in game) computers or computer programs to take them over. It's also streamlined, with all matrix (think futuristic internet) actions being reduced to two basic skill pools. It’s a lot quicker, and also means that you can do so during a fight rather than having to do all the hard work before a fight. Also, all the naughty things you do online make the tracking score on your dirty deeds go up MUCH faster. This means that a whole section of the game tracking how long until the matrix overwatch simply called GOD comes to smack you down now matters a whole lot more!

Mechanics or Crunch- Sixth edition is different, and that’s not bad! Overall, the mechanics are still the same. I liked 5th ed’s base mechanics, so 6th ed starts strong. The changes that have been made are all made in the name of speed. You don’t reroll init (speed), it's easier to do hacking (speed), magic is a smaller number of steps (speed), and a whole host of other things. These I really like. All the choices here seem to have been really well thought out. The things you won’t like are the loss of full crunch. Numbers are smaller and there are fewer fiddly bits. You can run a whole game of “Lasers and Love” where you do all the things in a Shadowrun game with just 1d6, but there isn’t a whole lot of crunch to that system. You most definitely don’t hurl a dumpster of d6s at a problem any more. That feels a bit wrong. And there is the armor thing…. It's small. The logic has been explained to me by both the creator of the system. It's mathematically sound. And it's wrong. It bugs me to high heaven that I can’t strap on a tank and take no damage from a pistol. I gain a point of edge (of the maximum 2 I can earn each turn) for the tank I’ve strapped to my face but still take a crap ton of damage if I can’t roll a dodge worth a crap! 4.5/5

Theme or Fluff- Shadowrun always has good fluff, and this book is no exception. That’s it. I’m in Michigan, and despite all the horrors of the 6th World, this is the one future where Michigan has an economy! 5/5

Execution- PDF? Yep! Hyperlinked? YES! This book is done pretty well. My main issues are some of the tables and the writing with them. A key example of this is the table for racial maximums. As you build a character, you are given extra points that you can put into special attributes like magic, edge, or some racial attributes like orcs are strong etc. You have to manually find that. It’s not hard when you jump onto the writer's logic, but why? Don’t make me five second logic puzzle out where my extra points can do. Just put that in the chart! There are a few times where those small puzzles come out. Now this is a MASSIVE BOOK, so editing issues will pop up, but those things hurt teaching new players this game. I live and breathe RPG, so that took me five seconds. A few of my friends though? It took multiple passes to make them understand if your score can go above six, then you can put the extra point there. But, for the majority of the book, this is how you want to make a RPG book. 4.5/5

Summary-The sixth sixth world is here, and I like it! This might not be a complete love affair as some minor things like tables and armor values murky my joy, but the changes together make a game that runs a WHOLE lot faster than before. The one thing Catalyst can do extremely well is tell a crazy story about orcs with shotguns in downtown Detroit, so the story and fluff of this game is amazing. The layout is good, with some minor issues that once you get past will work easily. Is this a perfect system? No, that can’t exist. The changes that happened all happened to make the game run on a non geological time scale. Will this make all fans happy? No. It’s got some differences that older fans just won’t grok. Does it make me happy? Honestly yes. I look forward to playing in the sixth world as my decker hacks Elf eyeballs in the middle of combat in Jersey fully as a member of a team even if my armor doesn’t matter much! 93%
top_rant.title: Ring Side Report-RPG Review of Shadowrun 6th ed.

top_rant.text:
THE STORY: I had recently watched “A Star is Born”, and so I already KNEW that I was going to get a Fashionistas doll that looked like Ally from the movie, but then this Pop Star doll caught my eye, as she had before, and I decided to finally just go ahead and get HER, TOO, as she was just too interesting to pass by!

I have absolutely NO REGRETS! This new doll takes stereotypical elements and surprising elements, and she marries them together in such a way as to create a whole new, refreshing standard for our ideas about what an up and coming pop star can look like!

THE AGE RECOMMENDATION….AND THE BOX: This very modern career doll is appropriate for anybody who’s 3 or older (In OTHER words, daydreamers, little kids, aspiring pop sensations, and anyone ELSE who finds this doll to be totally appealing!).

In all SERIOUSNESS, though, I can understand why one should only give this doll to kids 3 and older; Barbie arrives with a doll-sized microphone stand, and the included silver microphone, is removable from the stand. I wouldn’t want a toddler to be playing with that mic.

If you turn the box over, you’ll find a picture of Pop Star Barbie….and the following blurb by Mattel:

“When a girl plays with
Barbie, she imagines
everything she can become.

Pop stars are professional
musicians and performers.

They sing, play musical
instruments & dance!

Some pop stars write their
own music, too.

They travel all over the world,
touring & performing for all
of their fans.

Do you love to sing & perform?
You can be a pop star!”

Below the blurb is a reminder by Mattel that their customers have “200+ careers” to choose from; the URL for the the child-friendly Barbie website is ALSO included (kids.barbie.com).

ARTICULATION, SKIN TONE, AND BODY TYPE: This new pop star is none too articulated. :( Her head rotates, but she can’t really tilt her head very far in any kind of a downward direction. Her overall lack of articulation means, of, course, that she has almost NO articulation (except for the most BASIC type) in ANY part of her torso, her arms, or her legs. Her torso is COMPLETELY non-articulated. Her arms, which are ONLY articulated at the shoulders, can move forward, backward, and outward, and her right arm is permanently bent, so that she can hold onto the microphone stand….or the microphone itself. Her long legs (which end in high-heel-ready feet) are ONLY articulated at the hips, and they can move forward, backward, and outward.

Her skin tone is a rich shade of carob / caramel that could represent a range of ethnic backgrounds, although her facial features would suggest otherwise (a mostly Caucasian-looking nose).

I just compared this pop star girl with one of my Farmer Barbies, and, apart from the high-heel-ready feet, this girl seems to be a lot like THEM; she looks most like a Tall Fashionista to me! Her legs look to be a bit more long and slender than those of the Farmer doll, but her WAIST is refreshingly slight and realistic-looking, rather than being stereotypically pinched and perfect. Her rear isn’t all that large, and it’s covered by molded-on, flesh-toned underwear, which features tiny “Barbie” signature logos! This Barbie has a belly button, knees, and thighs that aren’t impossibly curvy.

NOTE: If you spread this doll’s feet apart, and balance her just right, she LITERALLY stands on her OWN….in HEELS!

FACE: This pretty pop star’s stage-ready look is surprisingly NATURAL. Her brows are brown, as is her eyeshadow. Her eyeliner is black, as are her eyelashes, but she has no lower lashes. Her blush and lipstick are sort of a taffy pink hue, and she sports a lovely “toothpaste grin”.

This particular Barbie has….SIDE-GLANCING EYES! She’s looking off to her left, and her irises are a mixture of two different tones (a golden hue in the center is flanked by two sections that are a lightish brown color).

HAIR: Some people are forever debating as to weather this doll’s hair is purple or pink. Well, I’m looking at her lip color, her microphone stand color, AND her hair color, and her hair is neither the color of her lips OR the color of her microphone stand. Her SCALP is actually kind of a purplish-grey color. Given all of these factors, and a glance at at least one online color chart, I’D have to say that her hair is some shade of….lavender; it’s a pink-purple color. I can’t actually TELL weather or not there are two different colors rooted into her head, though.

This girl’s lovely lavender hair stops at about a mid-hip level, and it’s straight….and sort of layered in the back, with NO curl at the ends. It’s parted on the left, and the shortest pieces that fall across the right side of Barbie’s forehead reach her midriff.

OUTFIT: Pop Star Barbie’s outfit basically looks just as it does in Amazon’s advertising photos. The top (velcro closure) is an ultra-sparkly silver color, with deep darts just below the bust and a scooped, almost halter-style neckline.

The skirt is an iridescent shade of silver. It features velcro closure at the back….and sewn-in white underpants, which do NOT look like thongs!

Anyone who is familiar with some of Ariana Grande’s concert outfits will know that this Barbie doll’s skirt was influenced by Ariana’s specific style!

Pop Star Barbie completes her look with “studded”, strappy, peep-toe high heels, which are a lovely shade of light purple, and which feature molded heart accents, too.

Oh, heck, though! They don’t want to seem to come OFF! : O

ACCESSORIES: This particular Career Barbie keeps it simple with no jewelry, etc., but she DOES arrive fully ready to practice….AND to perform for her fans! Included with her are a rather imaginative-looking microphone and stand set!

The mic. itself will fit on her right hand….with some effort. It is silver-tone. The base of the mic. is textured, and there are round accents around the base of the top of the microphone, which features a jewel shape….and tiny indentations for interest.

The stand that goes WITH the microphone has a magical, fanciful look to it! The plain, frosted, angled microphone holder sits atop the textured stand, which fakes the look of a beautiful column of light purple bubbles! The base of the microphone stand features a faceted look that lends another supporting element to the jewel theme of the entire microphone-and-stand set!

THE GOOD: There’s so much to love about this Career Barbie! Her overall LOOK is amazingly interesting. Her body type is statuesque, but not impossibly perfect-looking. Her interestingly-hued hair lends itself to a lot of different rehearsal and performance outfit ideas! Her facial expression is fairly common, but those SIDE-GLANCING EYES add an element of dreamy sass to her physical appearance. Even her skin tone is versatile, because it can represent a RANGE of ethnicities. Her makeup is pretty, but NOT cheap-looking or trashy. Her fingernails are defined enough that, if you want to, you can paint them!

She’s ALSO got accessories that bring her career alive! I love her microphone and stand set! So inventive and pretty!

I am glad that she can stand on her own, and that her microphone actually fits onto her hand.

I love the fact that the back of this doll’s box emphasizes the fact that a pop star CAN be a multi-faceted, multi-talented career woman, instead of just the pretty, shallow product of The Media Machine.

THE BAD: This expressive lady OUGHT to be ARTICULATED! She LOOKS graceful enough, but a POP STAR doll should be able to be POSED in a variety of different ways! After all, you SAID that some pop stars play instruments and DANCE, Mattel! C’ MON and give us some ARTICULATED Career Barbie dolls already!

It’s really TOUGH to get her to balance well enough for her to stand all by herself, and….her SHOES don’t seem to want to come off. :(

THE UGLY: It’s rather unfortunate that SO many of Mattel’s modern dolls suffer from rampant Quality Control issues. This doll is no exception. Your doll may have any one of the following flaws:

Plastic Issues: My doll has a few seams that are a little bit too obvious-looking / rough to the touch, and her legs feature thin lines across them, lines that separate slightly more SHINY-looking plastic from slightly more MATTE-looking plastic. Sort of AWKWARD, Mattel! Why do you do that to the legs of these poor dolls? Your doll may also have some random areas that look sort of….weird, with regard to the plastic (Streaks of matte-looking plastic, scratches, etc.)

Facial Screening Issues: I am REALLY glad that I chose my particular Pop Star Barbie in a STORE, because it almost took me a month of Sundays to find a GOOD-looking example of her! There were dolls that had pink lipstick paint covering the outside edges of their teeth. Some dolls had one rounded eye….and one more narrow eye, and a whole TON of them had wonky-looking reflective white dots in their irises. Some dolls, INCLUDING mine, featured slightly imperfect irises (My doll’s irises are slightly uneven in color, and one is missing a slight bit of brown paint….and is ALSO floating ever so SLIGHTLY above the lower eyeliner line). The whites of your doll’s eyes might look murky, too, and / or one iris might look fuzzy. In SOME ways, I almost can’t BELIEVE that I WAS even able to find a doll with a fairly good-looking smile, evenly-shaped eyes that were glancing evenly, etc.

Another facial screening issue that you might encounter has to do with Barbie’s blush! I swear that Mattel LOVES to play the “Let’s make Barbie up like a CLOWN!” game these days, because, even when it comes to MY doll, the blush ends in an OBVIOUS OUTLINE that looks sort of unnatural.

Hair Issues: Your doll may have thin hair, poorly-rooted hair, a happy-looking hairline, etc. I feel fortunate to have found a doll that escaped from MOST of these hair issues.

Stand Base Issues: The inside of my microphone stand base has large, ugly circles all over it….where it came off of the mold; because the microphone stand and base are TRANSLUCENT, the circles look very ugly.

Outfit Issues: Believe it or not, Barbie’s SKIRT can look rather unattractive….even in the BOX, and, if that’s the case, it’s gonna KEEP looking unattractive on BARBIE, long after the box has been discarded! You see, the fabric that was used to make the skirt can arrive with certain accidental creases and dimples in it, and you CAN’T really REVERSE these, because of the nature of the fabric; in other words, the skirt is KIND of like a piece of metallic Holiday wrapping paper, which, when accidentally creased, kind of looks ruined FOREVER. Barbie looks pretty SILLY wearing a skirt with AWKWARD, PERMANENT creases and dimples in it, because the skirt ends up sticking out strangely….because of the weird shape that its flaws can cause it to fall into. What a waste of a skirt that always SEEMS to be so pretty at FIRST. :(

ADDITIONAL IDEAS: I was SO displeased with Pop Star Barbie’s original outfit that I decided to give her a NEW outfit! I took the red rainbow / heart / star motif top from one of the Barbie Blind Bag Accessory Packs, and the pink “Love” necklace from another Barbie Blind Bag Accessory Pack, and I paired BOTH of them with the dark denim-look shorts that Chicken Farmer Barbie used to wear!

I LOVE Barbie’s new, casual look, and I have a feeling that a LOT of OTHER colors could be used to accentuate her lavender hair, her brownish skin, and the bright whites of her eyes!

IN CONCLUSION: This tall, interesting singing superstar is worth a LOT more than just a passing glance!
top_rant.title: This statuesque, Ariana Grande-style Pop Star Career Barbie stands on her OWN….in HEELS!  :)

top_rant.text:
The Walking Dead Card Game – Solitaire
By using the existing cards from the card game “The Walking Dead” you can create a solitaire experience with more drama and suspense than the original card game.
STORY
You are a lone survivor of the apocalypse stuck in the safe house, surrounded by Zombies and waiting for your two friends to show up. You need to survive through the night destroying ever zombie that attacks your safe house while also trying to rescue your two friends in the process.

VICTORY CONDITIONS
If you make through the night with one or two of your friends safely secure in the safe house, YOU WIN! If you are the only survivor you gain a partially win but if you are all eaten by zombies YOU LOSE!

COMPONENTS
[104] Zombie cards
[3] Hero cards

Each game turn consists of the following 5 phases:
1. DRAW PHASE
2. ZOMBIE SPAWN PHASE
3. HERO SHOOT AND/OR SURVIVOR MOVE PHASE
4. ZOMBIE MOVEMENT PHASE
5. DISCARD PHASE

SETUP:
1. Randomly choose your character “HERO” card and place him/her in the middle of the table; this will represent your HERO waiting in the SAFE HOUSE.
2. Take out the following zombie cards from the deck: 100, 101, 102, 103, and 104 and place them in front of you, these will be use later in the setup!
3. Shuffle the remaining 99 Zombie cards into one deck.
4. Deal out 42 cards into a single deck; this will be your AMMO SUPPLY deck.
5. Of the remaining 57 zombie cards, MIX in the other two HERO cards and the five Zombie cards; 100 thru 104 back into the deck. This makes up the new ZOMBIE deck.
6. Reshuffle the ZOMBIE deck and divide it into four even ZOMBIE SPAWN decks of 16 cards each.
7. Place the four ZOMBIE SPAWN decks in a cross formation around your HERO card leaving a THREE card space PATH around your HERO. “See example below”

8. The game is now ready to play!!

DRAW PHASE
Draw [4] cards from your AMMO SUPPLY deck and place them in your hand. If you have less than four cards in your hand draw till you have the max of 4 cards in your hand to complete the draw phase. If the deck runs out just reshuffle the discard pile to create a new AMMO deck.

ZOMBIE SPAWN PHASE
Turn over the top card in each of the four ZOMBIE SPAWN decks and place them on top of their corresponding decks, this is the zombie spawn area which you can’t attack because it is out of range.
If you draw one the follow:
1. A HERO “SURVIVOR” CARD – This is one of your survivor friends that you need to rescue and help get back to the safe house. To get the survivor to the safe house you must clear a path for the survivor to follow straight to the house. If the 3 card space between your HERO and the survivor has any zombies in it the survivor is not allowed to move into the path until it is clear of ALL zombies. If the path is clear the survivor can move during the movement phase a total of 2 spaces per turn. If one or more zombie’s moves on top of a survivor and are left there past the next SHOOT phase the survivor is killed and removed from the game. You CAN shoot at zombies in the same space as a survivor. Survivors can’t move if a zombie is in the same space as them. As long as the survivor is standing on the Zombie Spawn deck, no new zombies will spawn!! Immediately after the survivor leaves the zombie spawn deck, turn over a new zombie on the spawn deck.

2. ZOMBIE CARDS 100, 101, 102, 103, or 104 – These are special zombies who have somehow broken into the safe house! If one of these zombie cards is turned over on the zombie spawn deck they are immediately moved directly on top of your HERO card in the safe house; turn over another zombie card on the spawn deck to replace this zombie card. These special zombies must be attacked and destroyed first before any other zombie on the table. If any zombies on the safe house are allowed to stay there past the next shoot phase, YOU LOSE! If there is one “SURVIVOR” already in the safe house with your HERO card you have two turns to destroy the zombie! If there are two “SURVIVORS” already in the safe house with your HERO card you now have three turns to destroy the zombie! It’s possible for more than one zombie to break into the safe house at one time and be attack by multiple zombies.

HERO SHOOT AND/OR SURVIVOR MOVE PHASE
SHOOTING ZOMBIES
If any zombies are on any of the PATHS, your HERO can shoot and destroy them. If the zombie is still on the zombie spawn deck they are out of range and can’t be shot.
To shoot a zombie you must have the correct number of rounds equal to or greater than the number of rounds on the zombie card.
Any zombies that have broken into the safe house must be destroyed first before any other zombies on the paths!!
You can use different combinations of round to destroy zombies as shown below:
Example:
1 round ammo card can destroy a 1 round Zombie card
2 round ammo card can destroy a 1 or 2 round Zombie card
3 round ammo card can destroy a 1, 2 or 3 round Zombie card
5 round ammo card can destroy a 1, 2, 3 or 5 round Zombie card

And a 7 round ammo card can be used to destroy one of the 5 or 7 round Zombie cards OR two of the 1, 2, or 3 round zombie cards!!!
If no zombies are on the path your HERO has nothing to shot at this turn and the SHOOT part of the phase is skipped.

MOVING SURVIVORS
If a survivor is on the zombie spawn area and no zombies are on the path between the survivor and your HERO, the survivor can move 2 spaces toward the safe house during his/her part of the movement phase.
If any zombies are in the safe house no survivors on any path can move and enter the safe house until all zombies in the safe house are destroyed!!
Survivors can only move 2 spaces at a time so it will take a survivor 2 turns to reach the safe house.
Survivors can only move toward the safe house when the path is clear and no zombies occupy the path in between the survivor and the HERO.
If there is a zombie in the path it will automatically move toward the survivor on the zombie move phase instead of toward the HERO. If it reaches the survivor and stays there past the next shoot phase, the survivor is dead and removed from the game.

ZOMBIE MOVEMENT PHASE
During the zombie movement phase ALL zombies on the table “except for zombie in the safe house” move one space either toward the safe house or toward survivors if they are on the same path that can reach a survivor; this includes the zombies on the spawn deck.
If a zombie is on the same path that has either a survivor or one that is on the spawn deck, it will always move toward the survivor instead of the safe house. The zombie will continue to move toward the survivor until it moves on top of the survivor and then stops! Once a zombie is on top of a survivor, that survivor can’t move until it is destroyed!
When any zombies move on top of the safe house, they must be destroyed first before any other zombies on the paths!
Zombies that have moved on top of a survivor must be destroyed next but only after all zombies have been destroyed on the safe house!!

DISCARD PHASE
During the discard phase you can discard up to your entire hand or discard nothing if you like; it is your choice which ammo cards to keep in your hand through to the next turn.

WHEN A ZOMBIE ATTACKS THE SAFE HOUSE
Whenever any zombies break into or move on top of the safe house, you are immediately attack by those zombies. If your HERO is the only individual in the safe house at the time of the attack you have until the next SHOOT phase to destroy those zombies or you lose the game!
If one survivor is in the safe house with your HERO you now have until the following shoot phase “two turns” to destroy those zombies but if you are not able to destroy those zombie within the shoot phase of the first turn you lose the survivor and he/she is removed from the game.
If two survivors are in the safe house with your HERO you now have until the following shoot phase “three turns” to destroy those zombies but if you are not able to destroy those zombie within the shoot phase of the first or second turn you lose a survivor each turn and he/she is removed from the game.

WHEN A SURVIVOR REACHES THE SAFE HOUSE
Once one or more survivors safely reach the safe house you get to increase your AMMO draw by one extra ammo card each turn. Your hand size now becomes 5 AMMO card per turn. If you ever lose one or more of the survivors, you lose that extra AMMO card draw for the remainder of the game and your AMMO hand now returns back to four cards.
TO WIN THE GAME
To win the game you must safely rescue and protect both survivors until ALL zombies have been destroyed! If you lose ONE survivor you only earn a partial win and if you lose both survivors you LOSE THE GAME!

VARIATIONS
If you want to make the game more challenging added the following changes:
1. Add FOUR survivors to the deck instead of two and to win the game you must rescue and protect ALL FOUR SURVIVORS until all zombies are destroyed!
2. Change the SHOOT parameters to the following conditions:
a. You can only kill zombies with the same zombie rounds card; no combinations!
I.e. 1 round ammo card only destroys a 1 round zombie card and 2 round ammo card only destroys a 2 round zombie card, etc…
3. Survivors can only move ONE SPACE instead of two during their movement phase!
4. If any zombies move on top of a survivor that survivor is now instantly kill instead of getting a turn to try and destroy the zombie!
5. Or make up your own!!!
top_rant.title: SOLITAIRE RULES FOR THE WALKING DEAD CARD GAME!

top_rant.text:
THE FACTS: I am somewhat glad that I purchased this Talk with Me (TwM) Barbie doll, because she is interesting, if rather imperfect-looking. I am SOOO glad that I was able to get her at such a LOW PRICE (I am getting a second one, too, at this same price, and I'm hoping that she's prettier than this one), because, and let me be 100% honest, THIS DOLL IS OF LITTLE TO NO USE TODAY AS A TALKING DOLL; she's FAR better as just a bare-bones, interesting-looking Barbie doll, and / or a COLLECTOR'S ITEM!

People tend to complain these days about how..."this Barbie won't work today," and such, but what they don't realize is that weather or not they leave this Barbie in her box, THEY ARE GETTING A TOTALLY COOL DEAL ON HER, BECAUSE she's not really operational today. They should be thanking their lucky stars that they DIDN'T have to pay, like, $537.85 for a doll that WON'T WORK WITH TODAY'S COMPUTERS!

If you want a programmable, interactive Barbie that is ALMOST EXACTLY LIKE Talk with Me Barbie, but that works with TODAY'S COMPUTERS, check out Hi Barbie; she's available NOW, and even though she's EXPENSIVE, she can be used with TODAY'S COMPUTER SYSTEMS!

The truth is that I once FOUND this exact doll in a thrift store, bought her, and then later gave her away; I gave her away because she was missing her clothes, and because she had a few stress marks on her face, etc. In some ways, I wish that I still had her, too, because she had MUCH better hair than the NRFB example that I just got, and because her face paint looked better, too. However, I am GLAD that I am going to own two NRFB copies of this doll by Tuesday (This one, and the other one that I just ordered), because I LIKE her outfit, her computer, her necklace, etc.

IMPORTANT NOTES: Talk with Me Barbie is APPROPRIATE FOR CHILDREN 6 AND OVER, and she is from...1997 / 1998, so her "minimum system requirements" are as follows:

* Windows 95 or Windows 3.1
* Multimedia PC 486/66 MHz or better
* 8MB of RAM
* Double-speed (2x) CD-ROM drive
* Color monitor with 256-color graphic capability
* 16-bit Sound Blaster or 100% compatible sound card and speakers
* Standard mouse and keyboard
* Available serial (com) port for connection: 9-pin (DB25) serial port connector included / NOTE: A 25-pin (DB25) serial port adapter may be required for users with 25-pin serial imports

THIS BARBIE MAY NOT WORK WITH SOME LAPTOP COMPUTERS!!!

What does this mean? It means that IF YOU WANT BARBIE TO BE FUNCTIONAL, you may have to ask for assistance, with regards to MODIFYING YOUR MODERN COMPUTER, so that it can run the appropriate software to program Barbie.

COMPUTER PAPERS, ETC. THAT BARBIE ARRIVES WITH: I have NEVER TRIED to program this Barbie doll, but she arrives with a programming CD, a bright pink (32-page) User Guide, an "Important Information" note (orange paper), and a Software Registration Card.

A few button-cell batteries are included, but, if you think that you will SOMEHOW be able to program this Barbie doll, PLEASE COPY DOWN THE BATTERY INFORMATION (or find it on the box / in the booklet) AND THROW THE BATTS AWAY, as they are...18 years OLD now, and battery acid can burn!

Barbie also arrives with her own little "computer area", which consists of a pink floor, a computer, and a chair; you have to pop the chair into the floor yourself.

BARBIE'S BODY, FACE, AND HAIR: A collector might well love this Barbie, because her BODY is interesting and rather unusual! Barbie's entire body, AND her legs seem to be made of hard plastic (?). Her NON-TWISTING torso features a centered, 7-holed speaker (right underneath her bustline), NO molded-on underwear, a screwed-on battery panel (on her upper back), a "talk" button (at about mid-back level), and a tiny, deliberately-manufactured hole (in her back, right above her rear end).

Her legs are extremely stiff, and they DO NOT BEND at the knees! Her left leg is permanently bent at the knee, while her right leg is straight (Sort of a fetching pose, actually, but not very flexible!) Why did Mattel DO that? Well, they did that because each of Barbie's inner thighs is really a battery compartment; you can't make battery compartments out of bendable vinyl. When you turn Barbie over, the backs of each of her thighs features a big, half-moon-shaped groove; those grooves contain the battery compartment screws.

Barbie's arms are the permanently-bent Superstar arms.

This Barbie doll has one of the strangest, most interesting faces EVER! Since her MOUTH is able to MOVE WHEN SHE TALKS, her face has gears underneath it, and the "skin" that makes up her face is actually a soft, rubbery-feeling plastic, which gives Barbie a strange, ever so slightly "shiny-faced" look; this Barbie's facial shape, and her facial features may also look slightly strange and unusual. Anybody who owns a Fab Faces My Scene doll will know EXACTLY what I'm talking about ;) , because THOSE dolls' faces are just like that of Talk with Me Barbie, except that in the case of the Fab Faces dolls, the facial "feature" is a change of facial expression...instead of a simple motion of the mouth! PLEASE BE CAREFUL WITH THIS BARBIE DOLL'S FACE, AS IT IS DELICATE!

Barbie's hair is a cascade of mid-thigh-length blondness, and her bangs are curled.

A goldtone pendant is PERMANENTLY affixed to Barbie's neck, and a cheap, gold-hued ribbon is threaded through it; this ribbon's ends are glued together, but I just know that time will deteriorate the ribbon. At least the ribbon can be replaced, should it ever become lost.

BARBIE'S OUTFIT: For a late 1990s doll, Talk with Me Barbie wears BRIGHT COLORS! WOW! Her smart pantsuit is almost a Day Glo shade of SCREAMIN' HOT PINK! The fabric feels a wee bit cheap, but okay, and the pantsuit features straight legs, a top-stitched waistline, darts at the bust, and two gold, elastic shoulder straps.

Barbie keeps her look interesting with a stylin' gold and pink jacket, the inside of which is mustard yellow. This interesting jacket comes down to about waist-level on Barbie, and it features a hot pink collar (which can be turned down or popped), hot pink cuffs (each of which flares upward, ending in a gold button and a point), gold honeycomb overlay fabric (on the sleeves; you should be careful with this jacket, as I do not know how well the thin, honeycomb fabric will age), and decal and rhinestone detailing (The left side of the jacket features a pink nail polish spill, pink lips, pink lipstick, a black roll of film, and a pink hat, plus one iridescent, clear rhinestone; the right side of the jacket features a pink and yellow necklace on a white bead strand, a mostly white and pink purse with black accents, a pink and yellow makeup brush (?), pink and yellow sunglasses, a white pencil, and two rhinestones (one iridescent, and one pink).

Barbie wears round, metallic gold earrings in her ears...and a matching ring on her right hand; I LOVE the look of these "gold" pieces! Barbie also arrives with two deep, candy pink heels (those slightly Minnie Mouse-type heels that Barbie wore during the 1980s and 1990s).

BARBIE'S ACCESSORIES, ETC.: Barbie arrives with a pink brush for her long hair.

She also arrives with a..."computer area" of her very own! This consists of a blue-speckled light pink, plastic "floor" that is roughly 6.5 inches wide by 10.5 inches long. Out of this floor "grows" a speckled, pink foot-rest for Barbie, a speckled, pink "computer desk" (with slightly grey-blue top surfaces), a white keyboard, and a white computer (1997-style, of course, complete with a bit monitor and pre-attached "Intel Inside" and screen graphics stickers).

In order to allow Barbie to sit at her computer desk, YOU must attach the blue chair (with silvery-grey legs) to the "floor" by pushing it down into the opening in the pink plastic; you may have to be forceful, and please note that THIS CHAIR DOESN'T FIT VERY SECURELY.

NOTE ABOUT PLACING BARBIE AT THE DESK: The ONLY way to really let Barbie sit in at her desk in a COMFORTABLE-looking position is to rest her feet ON TOP of the foot rest that's located at the base of the back of Barbie's computer desk.

SPECIAL NOTE: Please note that there is ALSO...A LONG, BIG, CLUNKY WHITE CORD STICKING OUT OF THE BOTTOM OF BARBIE'S COMPUTER DESK; Barbie's desk was supposed to be able to pug into a real computer. You MAY wish to leave the cord folded up and twist-tied, so that it won't get in the way, should your child just want to play with the Barbie doll and the Barbie-sized "computer area".

THE GOOD: I LOVE Barbie's cool "computer area," and I LOVE the fact that this particular Barbie doll is so convenient to play with. Her batteries were NOT pre-installed (At least, I don't THINK that they were, because some batteries arrived WITH her), so I won't have to mess around with unscrewing screws, and removing dead batteries! Thank GOODNESS!

I like the fact that Barbie is sooo interesting! Her face is totally unique, her bangs are cool, her pendant is really pretty, and her pose really rocks! Her ring can even be changed out, depending upon which outfit she's wearing, and she can wear almost ANY low-backed Barbie outfit from the 1980s or the 1990s.

THE BAD: Okay. I CAN'T make this Barbie doll into an interactive Barbie; I am not personally too upset about that, but some people might be.

I would say that the only bad aspects of this Barbie are her lack of possibility, her delicate facial vinyl (If you have kids, DON'T let them play roughly with THIS Barbie doll...unless you don't mind seeing EXPOSED GEARS; her face is FRAGILE, although it's okay for reasonable levels of play), and the fact that her necklace ribbon might come unglued someday. Oh, and the fact that only certain low-backed outfits will work on her, because of her "talk" button...

THE UGLY: This is one of those Barbies that can bear tons of...ugly...issues, which are almost ALL due to Mattel's lack of quality control! First, there's that SPEAKER on Barbie's torso; she can't wear normal bras and bikinis without it showing.

Secondly, there's Barbie's hair! Right down the center of Barbie's scalp is a 1 cm strip of WHITE, UN-ROOTED PLASTIC (The frontmost portion ends in a tiny, deliberate HOLE in the top of Barbie's head), which her hair has to SOMEHOW COVER...Yeah, RIGHT! LOL! As they say, "Good luck with that!"; her scalp CAN be covered, but the look of her hair is awkward. Her bangs might also look a little bit weird (My particular doll's bangs were rooted in such a way that they tend to part in the middle; they just look weird, no matter WHAT I do with them!)

Barbie's face can also have MAJOR ISSUES! Her face may be PERMANENTLY (?) factory-stained with one or more black marks, although these are tiny. Her blush may be unevenly positioned, and her eyes may look sort of wonky / badly-painted; in my case, Barbie's eye shadow is more obvious on one eye than on the other, there is some brown in the white portion of one of her eyes, and her eyes just look different from each other, in general. Barbie's mouth may also look sort of weird, due to the expression that her facial vinyl causes her mouth to take on, etc. In my case, her lipstick looks a little bit strange at the corners of her mouth, and her teeth look to have been painted a little bit crookedly.

RECOMMENDATIONS: I can recommend this interesting Barbie doll to...

1.) KIDS 6 AND UP: As a bare-bones Barbie doll (NO BATTERY INSTALLATION, and NO PROGRAMMABILITY), this Barbie doll could be a lot of fun for a little kid who just wants blonde, pink-clad Barbie doll to play with. As long as the "computer area" cord is kept tightly twist-tied, a child can be safe while playing with this Barbie doll. If you want to keep your child EXTRA safe, WATCH them whenever they play with Barbie's "computer area", or grab your toolbox, and remove the cord altogether.

2.) BARBIE COLLECTORS: If you've always wanted to own Talk with Me Barbie as an NRFB doll, so that you can add her to your Barbie collection, here's your chance to own her at that savings-conscious amount that will make everybody else ask, "How the heck did you get her at THAT low price?"

3.) COMPUTER PARAPHERNALIA COLLECTORS: This Barbie doll is firmly rooted in the computer culture of the late 1990s, so she'd make an interesting addition to that retro computer room / museum.

4.) PARTY-PLANNERS: Why not surprise your litlte Birthday Kid with this Barbie...as a CAKE-TOPPER? She'd look SUPER, re-dressed in a low-backed, pink party gown of some kind!

5.) 1990S PARAPHARNELIA COLLECTORS: Talk with Me Barbie is about 18 years old, which means that she'll be 20 years old in another few years. If you collect items that truly reflect the state of American pop culture during the 1990s, then this may be the PERFECT NRFB Barbie doll for YOU!

IN CONCLUSION: While you CAN'T expect this Barbie doll to be functional TODAY, you CAN expect her to be interesting, unusual, and an example of what the late 1990s were like for Barbie, and for America, with regards to computer technology, anyway.
top_rant.title: You DON'T buy this Barbie doll so that you can have a talking doll. You buy her as a bare-bones doll, and / or a collectible.

top_rant.text:
Clank! In! Space!
2-4 players, Competitive

Overview of Gameplay-

For those wondering right off the bat how much different this is from the original Clank! I can tell you that it is very similar with some added (awesome) tweaks. Overall the biggest difference is going to be the space theme when compared to original Clanks! Fantasy dungeon theme. I’ll go more into those differences later on.

The way this game plays is in my opinion a masterpiece of game design. Disclaimer, I love the original Clank and low and behold I love Clank! In! Space! as well. Up to 4 players sneak aboard Lord Eradikus’s ship and plot to steal some of his precious artifacts and try to make it back to the escape pods to freedom. To do this you will draw a hand of 5 cards from your personal deck of cards, which, all players start with the exact same 10 cards in their deck at the very beginning so everyone is basically on equal footing. Then on your turn you play all of the 5 cards you drew and take the actions listed on them. The three main actions are Move, buy more cards and attack monsters. Any cards you buy from a row of 6 cards go directly into your discard pile and these all get shuffled back into a draw deck once your draw pile depletes. So over time your deck of cards gets stronger and you can effectively mold your deck the way you want based on what cards you choose to buy.

So ultimately you are moving around the tiled board trying to retrieve an artifact and then move back to the beginning close to where you started and get on an escape pod and get the heck outta there. All the while this is happening you are making noise aka Clank and adding your specific colored cubes to the Clank area on the board. Now remember those cards you bought from the row of 6 cards earlier? Well at the end of your turn you refill that row up with cards drawn from the general deck. If any of those cards have an emblem of Lord Eradikus on them, he enrages and attacks! You then add any clank cubes from the board to the included velvety bag and draw as many cubes as Lord Eradikus demands! Which is to say whatever spot his token is on his personal track on the board. Now there are already a bunch of black cubes in that bag which are a help to you. Over time you will end up adding more and more of your colored cubes based on how much noise you make, which is usually determined by the cards you play. If you draw out any of your colored cubes you are damaged and those cubes are placed on your personal damage track. Fill that up and you are dead.

However if you manage to make it to AT LEAST the docking area of the board before you die you can keep you acquired points. Not only that but player death isn’t the end in this game. The first player that dies controls the Clank bag from now on and draws from it on their turn, it’s a neat mechanic to speed up the end game if someone dies or escapes. Anyway, so once the game comes to a close, everyone counts up their points which are a smorgasbord of things including the units you have collect, the cards you purchased and the artifact and other small treasures you found. The person with the highest number is the ultimate winner!

Win Condition /Length-

I’m going to go on record and say I’m not the biggest fan of victory point conditions in games. However that’s not to say they are all bad by any means. Some games can do them right. The Clank! Series is one of them. There are a few different ways to acquire VP’s in this game but they are all interesting and thematic. It’s not just some track along the edge of the board. In this game you are collecting rare and exclusive artifacts and other treasures. You are buying cards to strengthen your deck and also as you are looking those cards over, you are making a judgement call on whether you want to buy that card that gives you those great abilities but is worth zero VP’s. The way they intertwine the VP mechanic into every fiber of this game doesn’t make the game feel like a VP game, and I love that.

The game takes a pretty nominal amount of time to play. For those familiar with the original it takes a little longer than that version, which is to say about an hour and a half generally. Of course as with most games the more players you have playing, the longer the game will last.

Setup/Takedown –

Setup is both fun and kinda nerve-racking in this one. So the board is broken into many different tiled pieces that you assemble. 3 of these pieces are the same every game but 3 others are different every game. Which is to say that the 3 in the middle are double sided and can be placed in any order so that creates a HUGE replayability factor with this one. There is also a Clank! app that has a neat board randomizer that auto changes it up for you if you so wish. The rest of the setup is pretty standard for Clank, which is you place down the row of 6 cards and create piles of tokens that you will be using. The board setup is by far the longest because even after you get it assembled you have to place all the major and minor secret tokens and artifact tokens around all over the board. Give yourself some time for the board setup before you play it.

Now to the nerve-racking part. The board pieces fit together SO snug that I am almost positive that the edges will become frayed after a few plays of this. You will want to be very careful with how you put these together and take them apart so not to damage the edge of the boards. Luckily they are some really thick and sturdy cardboard. Once assembled it does create a really cool looking shape of a space ship which just adds even more to the already awesome theme.

Components/Game board –

The components are looking good as ever and I really love the little player tokens in this game. Especially the little purple dude, with his giant ears. The tokens are all cardboard pieces and nothing spectacular per say but they also have a good thickness to them as well. The player cubes are all wooden and there are some plastic pieces as well. The Lord Eradikus token is wooden and the bag to hold the Clank! Is a really nice oversized bag which you can easily get your hand into. All in all the components are above average.

I talked about the game board a bit earlier. And even though I do think it will become damaged after a few plays, I still love it. It’s a really awesome idea to keep the replayability high and to be honest I think the art looks neater than the original Clank! board. Just the way the lines connecting the different rooms look are more streamlined and organized. The old one they looked more drawn on and it didn’t really grab me like these do. That’s an extremely small quibble in the grand scheme of either game however.

Box/Storage –

The box has a nice insert that is Identical to the original Clank! insert and works almost just as well. The spaces for the cards are the same with 3 dedicated spaces to keep stuff organized. There are a couple bigger spots to place all the tokens and the baggie. You will need to find some baggies as well to sort out and bag up all the many tokens. So since the board is broken into tiles it doesn’t just easily plop in on top of everything like before. Luckily though the biggest tiled piece is large enough to fit over all the cards to keep them in place which is a lifesaver. Because of this you can easily store this on its side if need be. The other tiles just lay on top of that and with the instruction manual the box lid closes all the way with no issues and very little gap inside.

Visual Appeal /Theme–

I mean it’s just great. The artwork on the board and the cards is fantastic and the theme is just as awesome here in the space version as it was in the fantasy version of the original Clank! The few changes they made to this version of the game really work to make the theme even more awesome. For example you are not just rushing in and grabbing an artifact and getting out anymore. Now there is a force field around all the artifacts, in fact around the whole back end of the ship. You must place these cool little plastic data cube tokens on two different data hub spaces in two different sections of the ship to bring the force field down before you can proceed. Not only does this add to the already stellar theme, but it enhances the gameplay by making the game more strategic. Not only that but it adds to the game length as well which can be a boon to some. It will give you more time to enhance and personalize your deck building.

Rulebook –

Sadly the rulebook hasn’t changed from the original game. Up the up side if you have played Clank! before then there is a nice little section at the front of the book that explains the few differences and those Clank! veterans should have little trouble learning the new stuff. Took me about 10 minutes to go over all the new stuff and start playing. However for those that have never played a game of Clank! before, this rulebook isn’t the greatest. There are a lot of confusing parts and some things that are not even really explained. You will find yourself more often than not flipping back and forth through the book trying to find something or reference something you thought you saw earlier. A prime example are the new blue gem tokens. There is little to no explanation of what those are or how they work in the instruction manual. How do you collect them? Are they spent like credits when you use them? Do you need more than one to use multiple cards that have the icon at the same time? None of this is explained and it wasn’t until I came to the forums did I find the answers to these questions.

Table Talk/Fun Factor –

I have a blast playing this. The table talk is decent but more often than not you do end up kinda in your own world managing your deck and what you are playing. Plotting out what you are going to buy and where you are going to move. The player interaction is pretty low other than when you grab an artifact or major secret that someone else was eyeing. That said I have so much fun building my deck and just chatting about the game with other players during play that I don’t even mind the low interaction. Even though the game is a competitive game, you are never attacking one another.

Optimal Player Count/Replayability –

This is one of those rare games that succeeds in all player counts. At four players there is more tension to get those major secrets and the market items. At two players you remove a few tokens during setup to keep that tension level high. There is only one of each different item in the market in a two player game so the first of the two that buys it, gets it! Also the solo play is AMAZING. They really upped their game from the first Clank! in this aspect. Utilizing the Clank! App there is an entire campaign mode you can play solo and it’s just the bees knees. It has a little story it goes through and takes cards away from the row after you take a turn. It keeps the tension really high as you play and even scores you at the end of each game based on how well you did. On top of that there is a mode that changes the base game up for multiplayer as well incorporated into the app and has a minion chasing you around the ship.

As I mentioned a few times the replayability is superb in this one. The Original Clank! has a double sided board to increase replayability but this one takes it a step further and has 3 different double sided sections for a total of 6 different sides that can be interchange into different positions and sides. Add to that the 100 different cards in the general deck that can be drawn and the 3 different types of faction cards in that deck that can be synergized together and you have a HIGHLY replay-able game.

Final Thoughts –

This game is in my top 5 favorite games. I love the deck building aspect combined with the board play. What’s surprising to me is that this game naturally has a few game mechanics that I don’t typically like in boards games such as victory points and low player interaction, however this game wraps such a compelling theme and incredible gameplay around those that I don’t even notice.

The game stays much the same so that Clank! veterans can easily swing in but changes a few things to still keep it fresh. It feels more strategic because of these changes but also plays a bit longer which could be great things to some. I do feel the game is a bit more complicated than the original as well as there are new card mechanics to learn. That said they are not THAT difficult.

For those wondering which of the two Clanks! I prefer……well that’s difficult. So the original Clank! is fast, easy to pick up and straightforward. It has an amazing fantasy theme and really scratches that itch for a deep dungeon crawl deck builder. Clank! In! Space! is slower, has more options with the cards and strategic. There are many more pathing options on the board which create more choices. It also does its theme very well but with this one being more Sci –Fi on a space ship. I think Clank! In! Space! edges out original Clank! but just by a tiny margin. I prefer the fantasy theme with the dragon but the mechanics and the strategy of dropping the force field in Space! is really amazing. It forces you to visit different parts of the ship and really draws you into the game.

That said both versions are excellent and I would for sure check out which ever one of the themes interests you the most. If you are curious about the original Clank! you can view my review of that game here: https://www.amazon.com/gp/customer-reviews/RJGQLU4M8CBRU?ref=pf_ov_at_pdctrvw_srp
top_rant.title: Clank! does it again! Superb!!
